Plan written: Engineer fixes the Amplify Hosting MIME/rewrite config (root cause is usually SPA rewrite catching .js assets), QA verifies.

This is a deployment/hosting MIME type error for ES modules. Amplify Hosting (or whatever server) is serving .js/.mjs files as application/octet-stream instead of text/javascript. Fix is a configuration change: add customHttp.yml (Amplify) or equivalent headers/rewrites, ensure build output uses correct extensions, and verify index.html references and vite base config. Single focused Engineer task is sufficient, with a QA verification pass.

Engineer is working: Fix the 'Failed to load module script ... MIME type application/octet-stream' error. Investigate the project root for Amplify Hosting config. Add a customHttp.yml (or update amplify.yml) at the repo root that sets Content-Type: text/javascript for /*.js and /*.mjs, and Content-Type: text/css for /*.css. Also verify: (1) vite.config.ts has correct `base` (default '/' for Amplify root, or set via VITE_BASE if subpath), (2) index.html script tags use type="module" with relative paths produced by Vite, (3) dist/ output has .js assets (not unknown extensions), (4) any SPA rewrite rule for </^[^.]+$/> routes SPA fallback to /index.html but does NOT rewrite asset requests. Do NOT rewrite .js/.css/.wasm requests to index.html — that is the usual root cause. Run `npm run build` until it exits 0. No TODOs or placeholders.
Now I'll create `customHttp.yml` at the repo root and ensure `dist/` includes it (Amplify reads it from artifacts baseDirectory).

Build passes. Created `customHttp.yml` at repo root with correct MIME types for `.js`/`.mjs` (text/javascript), `.css` (text/css), `.wasm`, `.svg`, `.woff2`, plus immutable caching for hashed assets and no-cache for index.html. Verified: vite.config.ts has `base: '/'` (configurable via VITE_BASE), i

This is a pure engineering diagnosis + fix, no design or QA decomposition needed. One Engineer subtask is sufficient.

Plan written. Root cause: `package-lock.json` is out of sync with `package.json`, so Amplify's `npm ci` fails. Single Engineer subtask: run `npm install` to regenerate the lockfile, verify `npm run build`, commit and push.

The Amplify build failed at `npm ci` because package.json and package-lock.json are out of sync (missing json-schema-to-ts, ts-algebra, @aws-sdk/* packages, fast-xml-parser variants, etc.). Root cause: dependencies were added/updated in package.json without regenerating the lockfile. Fix is mechanical — run `npm install` to regenerate the lockfile, verify `npm run build` passes, commit and push. This is a single-engineer task; no design or product work needed.

Engineer is working: Fix the out-of-sync package-lock.json that is causing Amplify's `npm ci` to fail. Steps: (1) Run `npm install` in the workspace root to regenerate package-lock.json so it matches package.json (this resolves the Missing: json-schema-to-ts@3.1.1, ts-algebra@2.0.0, @aws-sdk/eventstream-handler-node, @aws-sdk/middleware-eventstream, @aws-sdk/types@3.821.0, fast-xml-parser@4.4.1/5.2.5/5.4.1, strnum@2.2.3 errors from the lockfile). (2) Run `npm run build` and keep fixing until it exits 0. (3) Do NOT modify amplify.yml or remove pipeline-deploy. Do NOT commit amplify_outputs.json. (4) Once the build is green, `git add -A && git commit -m "Fix: regenerate package-lock.json to sync with package.json" && git push` to trigger a new Amplify deploy.
Build is green. Committing and pushing.
